response.justification: Maria brings nearly two decades of broad engineering experience, current Staff-level work on AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators, agentic tooling), and genuine high-throughput systems exposure from Sociomantic's ad-tech platform handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's must-have of strong Python is unsupported — Python does not appear anywhere in her skill set or work history, with her systems work concentrated in DLang and product work in TypeScript/Node. Evidence of directing technical strategy at scale and cross-functional stakeholder leadership is also thin, with most roles framed as hands-on IC delivery. Worth a conversation given the AI-native alignment, but the Python gap and unproven staff-scope strategy work are real risks.
strengths: Current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling matches the AI-native engineering environment
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong craftsmanship fundamentals — TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and a track record of teaching/mentoring
concerns: No Python listed anywhere despite it being a must-have; systems depth is in DLang, a niche language
Limited evidence of staff-scope technical strategy, stakeholder management, or driving decisions across an organization
Career shows breadth across many stacks and short-to-mid tenures rather than sustained depth in one distributed-systems domain
